task_name: FLS Training Lab Overview and Demonstration
cannot_score_reason: Video is an instructional/promotional montage showing multiple FLS simulators and training sessions in a lab setting, with product demonstrations and marketing content. No single task performance is being formally evaluated with clear start/stop timing.
video_content_summary: This is a MEDISIMU laparoscopic simulator training facility video showcasing multiple training stations, various FLS task setups (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, and Suturing tasks are visible), multiple trainees and instructors interacting with equipment, and product marketing slides at the end. There is no individual performance being timed or scored.

frames_observed.frame_description: Setup overview - multiple MEDISIMU training boxes arranged at table with tablets and instruments
Multiple trainees and instructors gathered around trainers, no individual task timing evident
Closeup of foam pad with surgical incision marks (consistent with Peg Transfer or Pattern Cut training)
Product marketing slides showing MEDISIMU V2, MEDISIMU, and MEDISIMU box-only product lines
Concluding frame with operating room setting and text 'At the end of the simulator training, its time for the real thing...'

confidence_rationale: Clear determination that this is instructional/promotional content, not a scored performance. No individual trainee completing a timed task with measurable penalties.

demonstrated_techniques: Multiple task station setup and organization
verbal_cues: MEDISIMU - Laparoscopic Simulator (repeated in title overlays)
At the simulation lab of the hospital
At the end of the simulator training, its time for the real thing...
teaching_content.common_errors_addressed: None explicitly — this is a promotional overview, not error correction
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: mixed - shows novice trainees and experienced instructors in a training environment

note: This video should not be submitted for individual task scoring. It is a facility tour and marketing material. If you wish to score an individual FLS task performance, please submit a video showing a single trainee completing one task from start to finish.
